message:
fix(robot): improve pane-base-index handling and bv triage empty dir fallback

Session structure pane detection (session_structure.go):
- Fix agent pane range calculation to use actual min/max agent pane
  indices instead of assuming control+1..maxPane, which broke when
  pane-base-index is 0 (panes numbered 0,1,2 instead of 1,2,3)
- Guard against empty pane list after parsing by checking PaneIndices
  length and minIdx validity before proceeding
- Set TotalPanes from len(PaneIndices) after filtering rather than
  from raw line count, which could include unparseable lines
- Fix empty-output detection: check trimmed string for emptiness before
  splitting (strings.Split on "" returns [""], not [])
- Relax classifyLayout NTM detection: remove hard requirement for
  ControlPane==1, now checks AgentPaneStart==ControlPane+1 to support
  pane-base-index=0 configurations

Env targeting info (env.go):
- Use detected session structure values (WindowIndex, ControlPane,
  AgentPaneStart) for ExampleAgentPane and ExampleControlPane formatting
  instead of hardcoded :1.2 and :1.1
- Compute actual min/max agent pane indices from paneIndices slice
  rather than assuming controlPane+1..maxPane
- Add safe defaults (windowIndex=1, controlPane=1, agentPane=2) when
  structure detection is unavailable

BV triage cache (triage.go):
- Resolve empty dir parameter to os.Getwd() in both GetTriage and
  GetTriageNoCache, preventing cache key collisions and ensuring the
  bv command receives a valid directory argument

Test coverage (session_structure_test.go):
- Add "base-index-0 NTM layout" test case verifying that a session with
  ControlPane=0 and AgentPaneStart=1 is correctly classified as NTM layout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
